L1.area: HTTP request body handling and middleware processing

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the S3-compatible gateway. When neither the Transfer-Encoding nor the Content-Length headers are provided in chunked uploads, the underlying HTTP framework assumes there is no request body and sets the body reader to nil. This causes a panic when subsequent middleware attempts to read the request body, leading to a denial of service.

L3.area: HTTP request body handling and middleware processing
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the S3-compatible gateway. When neither the Transfer-Encoding nor the Content-Length headers are provided in chunked uploads, the underlying HTTP framework assumes there is no request body and sets the body reader to nil. This causes a panic when subsequent middleware attempts to read the request body, leading to a denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: When chunked uploads arrive without Transfer-Encoding or Content-Length headers (e.g. through a reverse proxy), the HTTP framework sets the request body reader to nil, causing a panic when the authentication middleware attempts to read from it.
